reviews.review_msg: no assignments, graded only through midterm, final and quizzes that counted for participation. his lecture slides are handwritten a4 pages that can be hard to read at times, but are pretty comprehensive nonetheless. he can bea bit hard to understand sometimes, but he's pretty chill and is willing to explain whatever during office hours.

Highly recommend attending lectures as Christoph does many examples in class and explains things much more in depth than the lecture notes. Was always available for office hours and found his explanations great. Make sure to attend class and tutorials (tutorial answers are not available unless you go)! Textbook optional but may be helpful.
Ye gg don't take this
dont take him ever

He's a really nice guy but his teaching really lacks. His writing is very hard to read and provides no application in class only theory and in the tutorials he gives so many questions that the poor TA just breezes right through without explaining anything. I've attended every class and every tutorial and I still have no idea what's going on.

Christoph is a very nice guy and great prof. Exams were pretty tough, not that they're are unfair, but they're really long and the questions have multiple parts. My advice: DON'T JUST STUDY NOTES! Practice tutorial examples, and make sure you fully understand every question on practice exams/assignments. Grading was pretty lenient too.

He is a good person and careful. But his exams,, many rates are telling us. He taught easy things and i got that and kept doing w his materials like tutorials and previous exams but real exams are always much harder. He just gave A LOT OF midterm stuffs on the final. His hand writing was hard to understand at first but you can start decoding later.
The exam contains too much knowledge irrelevant to this course. Too much content about Econ 425 was taught. The difficulty is overly exaggerated.

Pretty good prof, was skeptical at first with the low reviews. Although Econ 201 is not hard, it definitely is tricky and needs more practice than other courses. The tophat quizzes were fair but had confusing formatting. I HIGHLY reccomend focusing mainly on the tutorial questions and shift away from the textbook.

Great professor! Although his lectures are hand written, he gives lots of examples which makes concepts easier to understand. The class is very math heavy but if you understand the concepts and perform above the average you will do well. Biggest advice is go to office hours! He will take the time to explain whatever it is you are confused about.
Chaotic lecturer
The worst professor I've ever have.
HE IS TERRIBLE

Hes a nice person, but his teaching sucks. he has a accent and he's hard to understand, and repeats useless information that we wont be tested on. He makes the concept and material harder than it is. The TAs are also bad and won't help; they are always late and just as bad at explaining and teaching. Top hat is easy and is pretty much attendance

Yeah I failed Econ 201 with him on my first try. The class is incredibly hard but he's actually a decent professor. If you just want to pass, better not take it with him.
He has Top Hat in-class quizzes to force people to go to class. The exams were extremely difficult but looking back on it, you just need to review notes and practice problems.

The professor prefers to use handwriting slides in class which is not very clear but if you go to the lecture every time, then you can understand the concepts. REMEMBER, if you want to success on this course, go to the tutorial every week, ask questions during office hours, also do all the practice he provided.

Do not believe the reviewer below this. One advice? If you are taking this course be ready for hell.... That is all my friends, may you survive. Not only the course materials but the prof itself cannot teach well.

His class is awesome, and he is a great person too. You have so much to learn from him. He is knowledgeable, rigorous and gives good  feedbacks. Very willing to help outside class. I might have changed my field if I took his class earlier. The only thing is, the class is not offered very year.

Finals are tomorrow.... but i heard from other students who took this course that he grades really strictly and especially the final. I am scared but might change my review later. The midterm it was graded really strictly, should give more partial marks for work... I say take Joshua Boitnott instead! Way better rating.

A lot of content to cover in a very short amount of time. Don't take this prof, he is not a bad person but he is a bad prof. The TA seemed like they did not know the materials themselves so I guess they hire about anyone. Most of the class is failing and if you are from beedie take BUS207 instead. The average is low always and the marking is tough.

Really harsh grader. Nothing that came on the exams were covered in class or tutorials. I cried a lot during this course. The midterm and finals are graded very harshly. You can barely get your marks up. I attended all lecture and tutorials, studied 10+ hours a week and still did bad. Covers nothing important in lecture.
He doesn't explain everything, only basics. Get ready to suffer.
His handwriting is poor, and he likes it...well, his exam was fine, but his lecture is boring, spends a lot of time on the material that was not in the exam, making simple concepts very complicated.

Christoph is a good prof. He knows the material well and the tests are mostly fair. However, sometimes the material is not conveyed well (i.e.) he would spend too much time on areas with little significance like changing the appearance of an equation. Also, tophat is a good idea but is not it.

It is no doubt that ECON 201 is an hard class, even harder considering it is not curved (but he adjusts the grades). Since I got a 34% on the Midterm after studying a kit, I was so scared I was not gonna pass but I was able to redeem myself during the final. So my tips are: focus on exercises , do tut exercises in advance and go to office hours!
wasn't as bad as I expected from reviews just study and fully understand mock exams and assignments and you'll be fine midterm and final average was about 45% but class was curved
Christoph is the best.

Not as bad as I expected (from reading other reviews). We got the lecture notes + audio explanation which helped, then the live lectures was a review of the notes, so the textbook was not needed. The audio + notes explanation was clear if you remember anything from ECON103 and Calc I. Only downside was having to buy a TopHat subscription ($30ish).

Difficult course. Perhaps he's not used to teaching online but his way of teaching is really hard to follow. A big part of the course is delivered through pre-recorded audio lectures and pre-written notes and his handwriting is horrendous. Recording is also hard to follow since he jumps from topic to topic. Does not go over problems in class.

This man has the ability to make a simple concept that you already understand impossibly difficult. he will use letters instead of numbers in his examples so that you focus on the calculations rather than the concept. He will also spend time explaining ideas that won't show up in the exam and are not applicable to the real world. Just avoid him.

Seriously made me consider switching faculties altogether. Easy the worst "professor" I've ever had at this school. We all went into midterms and exams completely blind and quelle suprise more than 50% of the class failed each time. Complete fool of a teacher, avoid at all costs if possible.

No notes online, writes his notes at every lecture with his bad hand-writing, hard to pay attention. Furthermore, his notes are very theoretical which usually does not help in doing practice problems. Gives practice exams for mid and final but won't guarantee whether you'll do fine or not. Practice problems are different from the exams.

His lecture is ok,  recommend do not skip class. This course is very logical of analyze economics. 30% of midterm and 70% of final. The format is like 4-5 short answer question and 1-2 long answer question. If you go to the class, the exam is not hard very reasonable.  I like his class, his write notes during the class. Notes very detailed.
Great Prof, Helps a ton

Christoph is a good prof. He is very clear about the course material. His lectures are well - organized. Do all the sample midterms and final, you will find the exams are pretty straightforward. However, the exams are super long. Most students can not finish all of them. So the key for getting a good grade on his course is to PRACTICE!
Rushes through material, especially at the end of his lectures.
the course is tough as well as the test, he had bad handwriting, need to practice the assignment which he gave us

Tests aren't so much tough as they are long. Do the assignments and practice exams. He's clear in lectures but as others said he is theoretical - this is why you need to practice (duh). Lectures are well organized and at a good pace but because of this he's impatient about questions in lecture. Go to his office hours for questions instead.

Hard professor! He will teach you the concepts in class but will not teach you how to solve the problems. The exercises he will give you during tutorials is too easy but prepare to suffer on the midterm and finals.

He yells A LOT out of frustration, the problem is he doesn't seem to understand how to listen as to what you're confused about. His midterms are fair, but his final is BRUTAL. I left with a headache and saw ppl crying. WOW. Enough said.
Very good professor that wants you to do well but still makes you work for it
I admit that you need to work really hard to succeed in his class, but he is overall a fair person and i found him really nice

don't let those who don't come to classes mislead you. Chris is actually a fine teacher. Come to classes; take good notes; DO tutorial homework. Textbook is easy to read. Shouldn't take more than 30min for each chap. Class average is low so it is extremely easy to get an A+. Gotta take good advantage with the poor quality of Econ students at SFU.

I have a 3.3 GPA and got a C minus in this course. Final was very long and difficult. Few ppl finished early.  Don't take this class with him. Saw him during office hours and found he gets frustrated with students.  He just writes notes but doesn't teach them well.

Cristoph's exams are so unbelievably difficult considering he does not go over HOW to do the problems in class, simply the theory behind it. On the final, there were multiple questions we had never even seen before and it was far too long for the allotted time. He seems to think that a 40% midterm average is acceptable. Avoid him at all costs.
His final is EXTREMELY hard! midterms are okay...pretty similar to assignments/old midterms...
Do the sample midterm and assignment and then you will be fine on his exam. Nice prof but has terrible hand-writing :(
very very hard final!be careful!

Not good.  Handwriting is too hard to read.  Not good at explaining things.  Gives you questions on the midterm and final that are way harder than anything you learned in class.  TA's not good either.  Only take him if you have no choice.
Overall fair prof. His hand writing sometimes hard to read and not much practice problems in tutorials but you are set if you do the sample midterms and final
Awful. Not excessively difficult, just no teaching skills.
He put 30% for mid and 70% for final, and not curve this class. His hand writing is hard to read.

The ratings here do not do Christoph justice. I took this class with him last semesterm, and I found his teaching great. He writes his notes during class, so it's easy to follow along; encourages questions; and the exams are based on practice midterms. However, a word of caution: there aren't a lot of practice problems for tutorials.

The exams are relatively easy (b/c he gives practice midterms which r similar) but the TA marking was BRUTAL. I stopped attending tutorials b/c TA was horrible at teaching. 1) Go to lectures, take NEAT notes. 2) Clarify notes w/ textbook 3) Do practice exams + assignments and understand how to do them and u're set.

Great Prof. He really hits the point in lecture. I'm not suprized at 1/3 of the class failing because they don't attend lecture or attend tutorials. His exams are almost identical to his practice questions and old exams. Can't expect to pass if you don't plan on putting in the work.

Christoph is a solid professor.  Go to every lecture and pay attention.  His exams are based from his lectures, which is closely related to the text. 2-25% midterm, 50% final.  He gives out practice questions 2 weeks before the exam.  Study them!!!
He does not like to help students nor he does not like them to do well. one third of the class failed, and he tries to show that econ is hard.

Christoph's lecture notes and practice assignments are very helpful for studying for exams. The textbook isn't really necessary. Sometimes he can move too fast. Make sure you go to lecture though. I got an A the second time I took this class, up from a D when I first took it with Christoph. The difference was going to class the second time around.

I think he is not bad. Comparing with another lady in summer09. He is relatively better than her. He didn't teach the general equilibrium on ch.30 and he didn't expect you to calculate the long run cost function which both could drive students crazy. Overall, he is not bad.
Brutal!! no chance on learning the material and doing well with this guy. Also, needs to find some new TAs! The "T" is meant to stand for teaching, not terrible!!
this guys is ridiculous! there is no way he can be a professor! avoid him at all cost!!!! one third of the class did not pass this course, what do u think?
i think his exams are fair, very very similar to the practice homework. Just work on the exercise and you'll do fine
36% of ppl got "F" after final
Should review calculus material. Try to help students but not the best at explaining things. Assignments too easy & exams wayy too hard!!
his hangdwriting really hard to understand and TA also so bad .. always make mistakes on writing and this is the hardest course I felt so terrible.

This is definitely a course worth taking with this prof. The class was rigorous but the prof made it easy by requiring only very few simple Math methods. Most of the problems on the exams were really straight-forward and the short-answer questions required only basic understanding of the notes. By far, an easy class. You learn a lot with this prof.
I always keep my opinions about profs to myself but, christoph is the reason i came to this website. By far the hardest Prof i have ever encountered.

unclear both verbal and handwriting. He teaches all theory and notation with no examples; Thus making his assignments and exams beyond hard. He also makes a lot of mistakes in his notes and rushes through them.

i am honestly tired of these kinds of profs that sfu keeps hiring. do they only hire hard/unclear profs??? this prof is the sole reason that i will not be majoring in econ. he completely discouraged me from ever concentrating in economics.
Do not take him if u have a chance

I have never felt so lost in a class as I do in his 301 class. He speeds through the course with no concern of whether or not the students are learning the material. Probably a nice guy, but definately a bad teacher!!

Terrible Prof. Assignments are hard, not related to his notes what so ever. His handwriting is hardly readable. Boring class, and it was just a disaster. Couldn't teach, not very clear, not to mention his exams and notes are completely different from Econ 302 but the materials are identical. Hard exams. Don't take him if u don't have to.
